body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: audit domain-humility (structural vs behavioral)
body.intention: I intend that audits are more trusted after the structural/behavioral distinction ships.
body.description:
**What changed:** audit-design and audit-ticket now categorize findings as STRUCTURAL (directive) or BEHAVIORAL (question).

**Predicted unintended effects:** (1) Behavioral findings phrased as questions may be easier to ignore — watch for real structural violations being mislabeled as behavioral; (2) The distinction may be ambiguous at edges (e.g., naming conventions — structural or behavioral?).

**Signals to watch:** Audit output post-change: are directives actually structural (wrong file, missing field, wrong abstraction)? Are questions genuinely behavioral? Any false negatives (real structural violations asked as questions).

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-20 or after 3 audit-design runs with the new skill active.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe; annotate as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** observation ticket; done when predicted effects annotated and ticket closed
